common.foot1: Cobijo Health — a California nonprofit (501(c)(3) in formation). This tool provides information, not legal or medical advice. Free & open-source. · hello@cobijohealth.org
common.mt_note: This page was translated with machine assistance and is being reviewed by native speakers. The English version is authoritative. Spot an error? Email hello@cobijohealth.org.

about.title: About Cobijo Health — who we are & how it works
about.meta_desc: Cobijo Health is a California nonprofit (501(c)(3) in formation) building a free, multilingual tool that helps patients get financial help for medical bills. Who we are, how it works, and where our data comes from.
about.lead: Cobijo Health is a California nonprofit (501(c)(3) in formation) building a free, multilingual tool that helps low-income, uninsured, and immigrant patients get the financial help they're legally entitled to for their medical bills.
about.mission_p: Every California hospital is required by law — the Hospital Fair Pricing Act — to offer free or discounted care to patients who can't afford their bills. Nonprofit hospitals must do the same under federal law (IRS §501(r)). But the help is effectively hidden: each hospital's rules sit in a separate, dense legal PDF, rarely translated and never in one place. People who qualify get sent to collections instead. Cobijo's job is to be the front door to help you're already owed.
about.how_p: You tell us your hospital, income, and household size. In seconds, Cobijo tells you whether you may qualify for free or discounted care at that specific hospital — with the income limit — screens you for Medi-Cal and Covered California, explains your rights if a bill is in collections, and writes you a ready-to-send request letter. It's free, it works in many languages, and it saves nothing you enter.
about.ai_h: How we use AI (and where humans stay in charge)
about.ai_p: The reason this can exist is AI. We use large language models to read hundreds of hospitals' financial-assistance policy PDFs and pull each one's exact eligibility rules into a single, clean, open dataset. AI does the reading and drafting; people verify flagged edge cases. Your eligibility for public benefits is computed with open-source public-benefit rules (PolicyEngine), not guessed. AI helps — it never makes the final decision for you, and only the hospital can approve your assistance.
about.src1: California's official hospital financial-assistance policy registry (HCAI)
about.src2: California's Hospital Fair Pricing Act (Health & Safety Code §127400 et seq.)
about.src3: Federal charity-care requirements for nonprofit hospitals (IRS §501(r))
about.src4: Medi-Cal and Covered California eligibility rules (via PolicyEngine)
about.src_note: Everything Cobijo builds — the dataset and the tools — is open-source and open-data, so anyone can check our work or build on it.
about.team_p: Cobijo Health was founded by Ian Duncan, who builds the product end to end. We're recruiting an independent founding board — a nonprofit/health attorney, a CPA/treasurer, a healthcare-access expert, a fundraiser, and a community member with lived experience — and filing for 501(c)(3) status. If you'd like to help, we'd love to hear from you.
about.involve_lead: Cobijo Health is early, open, and built in the open. There are three ways to help — all real, none requiring money.
about.involve_board_p: We're recruiting five independent directors as we file for 501(c)(3) status: a nonprofit or health attorney, a CPA or treasurer, a healthcare-access expert, a fundraiser, and a community member with lived experience of medical debt. A few hours a quarter, real impact. Email us.
about.involve_help_p: We especially need native speakers to review our translations — we serve 10 languages, currently machine-assisted. We'd also love to hear from clinics, legal-aid groups, and community organizations who reach the patients we're built for. Email us.
about.involve_support_p: Everything — the tools and the dataset — is free and open-source. Star or contribute to the project on GitHub — it's a genuine public good, and every bit of visibility helps patients find it.
about.disc_card: Cobijo Health gives you information, not legal, medical, financial, or tax advice. Results are estimates based on what you enter and public hospital policies — they are not a decision or a guarantee. Only the hospital can decide if you qualify. Cobijo is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or acting on behalf of any hospital.

privacy.title: Privacy — Cobijo Health
privacy.meta_desc: Cobijo Health does not save your answers. What you enter is used only to calculate your result, in the moment, and is never stored, sold, or shared.
privacy.summary: Short version: We don't save your answers. Ever.
privacy.intro: Cobijo Health is a free tool that helps you find out if you may qualify for hospital charity care, and creates a request letter you can use. We built it to protect your privacy.
privacy.collect_p: To give you a result, we ask for your hospital, your income, your household size, whether you have insurance, and whether a bill is in collections.
privacy.use_p: We use your answers only to calculate your result and letter, right then, in the moment. As soon as we show you the result, your answers are gone.
privacy.dont1: We don't save your answers in any database.
privacy.dont2: We don't create an account or ask for your name.
privacy.dont3: We don't sell, rent, or share your answers with anyone.
privacy.dont4: We will never share what you enter with your hospital, the government, or immigration.
privacy.hipaa_p: Cobijo Health is not a doctor, hospital, or insurance company. That means HIPAA — the medical privacy law — does not apply to this tool. We protect you a different, stronger way: by not storing your answers at all.
privacy.analytics_p: We use privacy-friendly analytics (Plausible) to count how many people visit our pages. It uses no cookies and collects no personal information. It cannot see your form answers.
privacy.third_p: Our website is delivered through standard hosting and security services (such as Cloudflare). These help the site load safely. They do not receive your form answers.
privacy.changes_p: If we change this policy, we'll update it on this page and change the date below.
privacy.nonprofit_note: Cobijo Health is a California nonprofit (501(c)(3) in formation).

faq.title: FAQ — Cobijo Health
faq.meta_desc: Answers to common questions about Cobijo Health: Is it free? Is my information safe? Will getting help affect my immigration case? What if I'm undocumented or my bill is in collections?
faq.intro: Honest answers to the questions people ask most. Still stuck? Email hello@cobijohealth.org.
faq.a1: Yes. Cobijo Health is completely free to use, with no catch. We're a nonprofit, not a business — we don't charge you, and we don't take a cut of anything you save. There's nothing to buy and no account to create.
faq.q2: Is my information safe? Do you save what I enter?
faq.a2: We don't save your answers at all. What you type is used only to calculate your result in the moment, then it's gone — there's no account, no database of your answers, and we never sell or share them. We will never share what you enter with your hospital, the government, or immigration. See our Privacy page for details.
faq.q3: Will getting help hurt my immigration case ("public charge")?
faq.a3: For most people, no. Under current federal rules, using Medi-Cal, CHIP, Covered California, emergency care, or a hospital's financial-assistance (charity care) program does not count against you in a "public charge" immigration test — and hospital charity care is not a government benefit at all. Public charge also does not apply to many people, including green-card renewals, citizenship applicants, and refugee, asylee, U-visa, T-visa, and VAWA cases. This is general information, not legal advice, and immigration rules can change. For your specific situation, please talk to a trusted immigration attorney or a nonprofit legal-aid organization before making a decision.
faq.q4: Can I use this if I'm undocumented?
faq.a4: Yes. Hospital charity care is based on income and household size, not immigration status, and California hospitals must offer it to everyone who qualifies. In California, Medi-Cal is also available to income-eligible people regardless of immigration status, though enrollment rules can change over time. We don't ask for your immigration status and we don't save your answers.
faq.q5: How accurate is this? Is it a guarantee?
faq.a5: Cobijo gives you an estimate based on the information you enter and each hospital's own published financial-assistance policy. It is not a decision and not a guarantee. Only the hospital can approve your assistance — always confirm with the hospital's financial-assistance office. If we ever aren't sure about a hospital's policy, we tell you.
faq.q6: What if my bill already went to collections?
faq.a6: You may still have options. In many cases you can apply for charity care even after a bill has gone to collections, and California law gives you rights when a hospital bill is being collected. Cobijo explains those rights and can point you to free legal aid.
faq.q7: Do I need insurance to use this?
faq.a7: No. Cobijo is built especially for uninsured and self-pay patients, but insured patients with high out-of-pocket bills can qualify for help too. Enter your situation and we'll tell you what may apply.
faq.q8: Who's behind Cobijo, and is it a real nonprofit?
faq.a8: Cobijo Health was founded by Ian Duncan and is a California nonprofit in the process of forming (501(c)(3) status pending). It's open-source and built in the public interest. Learn more on our About page.
faq.a9: Cobijo works in English, Spanish, Chinese, Vietnamese, Tagalog, Korean, Armenian, Farsi, Arabic, and Russian. Use the language menu at the top of any page to switch.

plan.cc_free: You likely qualify for **FREE care**. {name} gives free/full charity care up to {free:.0f}% of the Federal Poverty Level{upto}, and your household is at ~{pct:.0f}%.
plan.cc_free_upto: (free for a household of {household} earning up to ~${ceil:,}/yr)
plan.cc_discount_tier: You likely qualify for a **DISCOUNT**. Your ~{pct:.0f}% FPL falls in this hospital's {lo:.0f}–{hi:.0f}% band: {how}.
plan.cc_discount_ceiling: You're within {name}'s discount-eligible range (up to {disc_ceiling:.0f}% FPL) — apply for the sliding-scale discount.
plan.cc_high_cost: You have insurance, but {name} has a **high-medical-cost** provision: if your out-of-pocket bills exceed ~{thr:.0f}% of your income you may still qualify for help. Worth applying.
plan.cc_over: Your income (~{pct:.0f}% FPL) is above {name}'s assistance ceiling (~{ceiling:.0f}% FPL) — but still apply (policies changed in 2025), and use bill-error review, negotiation, and the options below.
plan.ben_insured: You reported having insurance — we'd still check for cost-sharing help and secondary programs.
plan.ben_medicaid_heuristic: **Medi-Cal (California Medicaid)** — at ~{pct:.0f}% FPL you're likely income-eligible for free or low-cost coverage that can even apply to recent bills. California covers many adults regardless of immigration status, though enrollment rules changed in 2026 — check your current eligibility. Using Medi-Cal does not count against a public-charge immigration test.
plan.ben_aca_heuristic: **Covered California (ACA) subsidies** — at ~{pct:.0f}% FPL you likely qualify for premium help. (Enhanced subsidies were set to lapse after 2025 — verify 2026 rules; PolicyEngine reflects current law.)
plan.ben_over_heuristic: Income may be above Medi-Cal/subsidy lines — we'd still check county indigent-care and special programs.
plan.pe_medicaid: **Medi-Cal (California Medicaid)** — current CA rules (via PolicyEngine) show your household likely eligible for free/low-cost coverage worth an estimated ~${value:,}/yr. California covers many adults regardless of immigration status (enrollment rules changed in 2026 — check your current eligibility), and coverage can apply to recent bills. Using Medi-Cal does not count against a public-charge test.
plan.pe_aca: **Covered California (ACA) subsidies** — PolicyEngine estimates a premium tax credit of ~${ptc:,}/yr to lower your monthly premium on a marketplace plan.
plan.pe_over: Based on current CA rules (PolicyEngine), your income appears above the Medi-Cal and marketplace-subsidy lines — we'd still check county indigent-care and special programs.
plan.debt_rights: Because a bill is in collections: you have rights under the FDCPA and CA law — you can dispute the debt and request validation.
plan.debt_retroactive: Charity care can often be applied **retroactively** to wipe a bill already in collections — do that first.
plan.debt_legalaid: For any lawsuit or wage-garnishment threat, we'll connect you to **free legal aid** (we don't give legal advice).
plan.debt_credit_note: Note (2026): the federal rule removing medical debt from credit reports was struck down, but bureaus still drop paid collections and those under $500.
plan.plan_greeting: Hi {first} — here's your personalized plan for the bill from {name}.
plan.plan_household: (Household of {household}, income ${income:,}/yr ≈ {pct:.0f}% of the Federal Poverty Level.)
plan.plan_needs_review: [⚠ This hospital's data is flagged for staff verification — treat the numbers as a strong lead, not final.]
plan.step1: STEP {n} — Hospital charity care (biggest lever, free to apply):
plan.step1_apply_phone: Apply through the hospital business office: {phone}. We'll generate the request letter for you (below).
plan.step1_apply_nophone: We'll generate the request letter for you (below) — send it to the hospital's Financial Assistance / Business Office.
plan.step1_retroactive: You can apply even if the bill already arrived or went to collections (retroactive).
plan.step1_interest_free: If a balance remains, this hospital offers an **interest-free payment plan** — ask for it by name.
plan.step2: STEP {n} — Coverage you may qualify for going forward:
plan.step3: STEP {n} — Protecting you from the debt:
plan.step4: STEP {n} — If a balance remains: bill-error review, payment plan, disease-specific funds, and (last resort) a coached crowdfunding campaign.
plan.letter_note_english: Note: the request letter below is written in English so the hospital's billing office can process it quickly.
plan.result_free: Good news — you likely qualify for FREE care
plan.result_discount: You likely qualify for a discount
plan.result_high_cost: You may qualify based on high medical costs
plan.result_over: You may be over the income limit — but you still have options
plan.cc_unknown: We couldn't read {name}'s exact income limits from its policy — but every California hospital is required by law to offer free or discounted care. **Apply anyway** — at ~{pct:.0f}% of the Federal Poverty Level you may well qualify.
plan.result_unknown: We couldn't confirm this hospital's limits — but you should still apply
plan.cc_no_hospital: We don't have this hospital's details in our directory yet — but every California hospital is required by law to offer free or discounted care. **Apply anyway** — at ~{pct:.0f}% of the Federal Poverty Level you may well qualify.

landing.title: Cobijo Health — free help with your medical bills
landing.meta_desc: Cobijo Health is a free, multilingual tool that helps low-income and uninsured patients in California get financial help for medical bills: hospital charity care, benefit screening, and medical-debt defense.
landing.h1: Millions of Californians qualify for free hospital care — and never get it.
landing.lead: Every California hospital is required by law to offer free or discounted care to patients who can't afford their bills. Cobijo Health finds that help for you — in plain language, in your language, for free.
landing.free: Free · multilingual · nothing is saved · not legal or medical advice
landing.how_h: Three ways we help with one bill
landing.how_s: Tell us your hospital, income, and household size. In seconds you get a personalized plan.
landing.st1_p: Find out if you qualify for free or discounted care at that specific hospital — with the exact income limit — and get a ready-to-send request letter.
landing.st2_p: We screen you for Medi-Cal and Covered California — coverage you may qualify for that can even apply to recent bills.
landing.st3_p: Know your rights if a bill is in collections, apply charity care retroactively, and get connected to free legal aid.
landing.who_s: The people the system leaves to decode a 20-page policy alone.
landing.who1: Uninsured and self-pay patients facing a bill they can't afford
landing.who2: Low-income and immigrant families — California covers eligible adults regardless of immigration status
landing.who3: Anyone whose bill went to collections before they knew help existed
landing.who4: Patients who've never seen this explained in their own language
landing.moat_p: Every hospital's charity-care rules are buried in a separate, dense legal PDF. No one had ever put them in one place. Cobijo uses AI to read California hospitals' financial-assistance policies and extract each one's exact rules into one clean, open dataset — so the tool can tell you what your hospital actually offers. Open-source and open-data, built in the public interest.
landing.band_h: Ready to see what you qualify for?
landing.band_p: It takes about a minute, it's completely free, and we don't save anything you enter.
landing.tagline: This tool provides information, not legal or medical advice. Free to use. Open-source.

hospital.h_verify: ⚠ This hospital's data is being verified — a strong lead, not final
hospital.h_lead: Under California law (the Hospital Fair Pricing Act), {name} must offer free or discounted care to patients who can't afford their bills. Here's who qualifies and how to apply — you can apply even if your bill already went to collections.
hospital.h_free_h: Do you qualify for FREE care?
hospital.h_free_p: {name} provides free (charity) care to patients at or below {pct}% of the Federal Poverty Level. If your yearly household income is at or below the amount for your household size, you likely qualify for 100% free care:
hospital.h_disc_p: Patients up to {pct}% of the Federal Poverty Level qualify for discounted care — you'd owe no more than the amount generally billed to insured patients:
hospital.h_call_script: When you call, say: “I'd like to apply for financial assistance (charity care) for a hospital bill. Can you send me the application and tell me which documents I need?”
hospital.h_effective: Based on this hospital's policy effective {date}.
hospital.h_cta_p: Answer 3 quick questions and get a personalized plan plus a ready-to-send request letter — free, in your language.
hospital.h_meta: Find out if you qualify for free or discounted hospital care at {name}. Income limits, required documents, the application form, and how to apply — free.
hospital.h_foot: Cobijo Health is a free nonprofit tool (California 501(c)(3) in formation). We are not affiliated with this hospital and this is general information, not legal, medical, or financial advice. Always confirm details in the hospital's official policy. Data sourced from California's HCAI Hospital Fair Pricing filings.
hospital.h_dir_title: California Hospital Financial Assistance & Charity Care Directory | Cobijo Health
hospital.h_dir_meta: Browse financial assistance and charity care programs for every California hospital. Free income limits, application forms, and how to apply — from Cobijo Health.
hospital.h_dir_lead: Every California hospital must offer free or discounted care to patients who can't afford their bills. Find your hospital below to see its income limits and how to apply — or check your specific bill.
